description:
You are a network engineer who needs to document a quick connectivity-performance snapshot from the current machine to itself.  
    1. Create a directory called /home/user/network_logs (if it does not already exist).  
    2. Run a latency benchmark consisting of exactly four ICMP echo-requests to the loopback interface (127.0.0.1).  
       • Use any standard CLI tool that ships in a minimal Linux install (e.g., “ping”).  
       • Make sure only four probes are sent.  
    3. From the command’s stdout, copy **only** the two summary lines that appear after the probe sequence completes and save them to a text file called /home/user/network_logs/ping_test.log.  
       • Line-1 must be the packet-summary line that starts with “4 packets transmitted”.  
       • Line-2 must be the round-trip statistics line that starts with “rtt min/avg/max/mdev”.  
       • Do NOT include any of the individual per-probe lines, timestamps, or blank lines.  

    The final /home/user/network_logs/ping_test.log must therefore contain exactly two lines, each terminated with a newline character “\n”.  
    Example layout (numbers will vary and are **not** hard-coded):  
        4 packets transmitted, 4 received, 0% packet loss, time 3058ms  
        rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 0.021/0.024/0.031/0.004 ms  

    Important: keep the wording and spacing of the two lines exactly as produced by the tool; only the numeric values may differ.  
    A subsequent automated test will:  
        • Verify that the directory /home/user/network_logs exists.  
        • Verify that /home/user/network_logs/ping_test.log exists and is readable.  
        • Check that the file contains exactly two newline-separated lines.  
        • Validate that the first line begins with the literal string “4 packets transmitted”.  
        • Validate that the second line begins with the literal string “rtt min/avg/max/mdev”.  

    No other output, files, headers, or commentary should be added to ping_test.log.
truth:
Pre-task state:
        • No /home/user/network_logs directory exists.
    
    Post-task ground truth required by the grader:
        1. Directory /home/user/network_logs must exist (permissions 755 or more liberal).  
        2. File /home/user/network_logs/ping_test.log must exist, regular text file, world-readable.  
        3. The file must have exactly two newline-terminated lines:
              REGEX for line-1: ^4 packets transmitted, 4 received, 0% packet loss, time [0-9]+ms\n$
              REGEX for line-2: ^rtt min/avg/max/mdev = [0-9]+\.[0-9]{3}/[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3}/[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3}/[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3} ms\n$
        4. No extra bytes before, between, or after those two lines.  
        5. Total file length must therefore be: len(line1) + len(line2).  
        6. The agent does not need elevated privileges; ping to 127.0.0.1 is allowed inside the container.
